project.name: dns-poisoning-lab
project.purpose: Controlled Docker labs for simulating DNS cache poisoning attacks and POPS-style mitigation rules.
project.safety_boundary: All attacks are intended to run only inside isolated Docker bridge networks.

client.role: Generates DNS queries, triggers resolver cache misses, records result.txt and latency_ms.txt.

resolver.role: Simulated recursive resolver with cache, upstream query logic, and toggleable defense rule.

auth.role: Authoritative DNS server that returns legitimate DNS answers with controlled delay.

attacker.role: Sends spoofed DNS responses or forged fragment-like responses toward the resolver.

stype.defense_summary: Rl1 uses stronger TXID/source-port entropy when enabled and returns TC=1 when suspicious mismatched responses are observed.

oob.attack_summary: Attacker races spoofed DNS responses containing an additional out-of-bailiwick A record for bank.com.
oob.defense_summary: Rl3 checks bailiwick and returns TC=1 when an out-of-bailiwick record is observed.

sfrag.attack_summary: Attacker floods forged frag2-like responses over an IPID search space.
sfrag.defense_summary: Baseline Rl2 blocks/truncates when a fragment marker is detected.

bfrag.attack_summary: Attacker knows or fixes the target IPID and repeatedly injects a matching frag2-like response.
bfrag.defense_summary: Baseline Rl2 blocks/truncates when a fragment marker is detected.

r2entropy.defense_summary: Improved Rl2 records offset>0 frag2-like events, computes IPID entropy, allows benign fragmentation, and returns TC=1 only when entropy and unique-ratio indicate a flood.

evidence_goal.benign_on: Show legacy Rl2 would block on any frag1 while improved Rl2 allows benign fragmented traffic.
evidence_goal.attack_on: Show improved Rl2 blocks high-entropy SFrag-like floods.

files.run_case_common.sh: Docker preflight, compose wrapper, stack startup, defense toggling, attacker worker control, metric collection.
files.measure_asr.sh: Copies result.txt from client and computes poisoning success rate.
files.measure_latency.sh: Copies latency_ms.txt from client and computes avg/p95 latency.
files.benchmark_case.sh: Runs a case repeatedly and averages ASR/latency.

baseline: client sends legitimate DNS query to resolver
resolver forwards cache miss to auth
client records bank.com answer and latency
attack_off: attacker floods spoofed DNS responses or frag2-like responses
resolver may accept forged data when defense is disabled
client records whether bank.com resolves to 6.6.6.6
attack_on: resolver either allows benign traffic or returns TC=1 / blocks suspicious poisoning
artifacts record metrics and detailed logs
